review_text: Hated Scarlett’s character at first. Why was she making it hard for him almost on purpose. In the end I understand it.
It was great seeing Scarlett and Adam Driver together, but the story fell rather flat and felt uninteresting. I would have much preferred a story in which they got back together lol.

Everything about this movie really worked for me while I was watching it. The amazing performances that truly made me feel like they were a real couple throughout the whole movie. The brutal fights between them which show how much they have grown apart. And even the rare beautiful, peaceful moments where they would stop fighting and were happy for each other. But the best thing about the movie is how it does not picture either of them as a monster, but as real people with their struggles. Which shows why this movie is amazing and truly understands how marriages actually work.

The performances are impeccable, and the script is exactly what I need to stop thinking about my family problems. It explores the theme of human relationships in a way that feels so intimate and profound that it's impossible not to be moved. Simplicity is the film's aesthetic, which makes it feel so warm. For me, it was a bit long... some scenes could have been shorter, and others cut altogether. The theater scenes and those related to work felt a little out of place with the overall tone, which isn't particularly opulent. Aside from that, while the premise isn't groundbreaking, the story's development will definitely stay with you. And the final scene will probably be hard to get over.
Great acting and storyline!
86/100 - amazingly portrayed story

Ah, este filme! Como gostei dele! Talvez pela excelência do elenco (e, graças a ele, tive o privilégio de conhecer a força da atuação de Adam Driver), somado a um roteiro verdadeiramente denso, um enredo que exige a total imersão do espectador. A produção se caracteriza por longos takes, diálogos que parecem intermináveis, o que apenas reforça o profissionalismo de todos os envolvidos. A cena de Johansson conversando com a advogada, enquanto explica a ela — e a nós — o que levou a situação a esse ponto, é um espetáculo por si só. Além disso, o ápice do filme, a discussão entre os personagens, é de arrepiar. E quando digo "arrepiar", é com "A" maiúsculo. Cada elemento do cenário contribui para a construção da tensão. Cada movimento, cada gaveta aberta, cada gesto, cada olhar. A profundidade da cena no tribunal, onde ambos aparecem de forma deliberada, sempre no mesmo plano, e ainda assim não se olham. Juntos, mas distantes, envergonhados, uma distância que é maior do que a física. Este filme é, sem dúvida, uma verdadeira aula de cinema: storytelling, roteiro, direção — tudo! O final, seco e realista, sugere que a vida seguiu seu curso, mesmo depois que paramos de assistir. Cinco estrelas, sem dúvida. Não tem jeito!

Marriage Story admittedly presents an emotionally realistic picture of divorce and the general reality beyond the end of a serious relationship. It realy doesnt pull any punches or fluff anything up. All the anger, delight and laughs youll feel are real because you understand each part personally.

A really sad movie. Portrays the broken justice system. The man can't hire the same lawyer that his wife also consulted with for the divorce. The man has to pay some of the wife's lawyer fees for divorce. This is the society's failure. So much money lost during divorces and so many families ruined...
Success in 2 hours and 16 minutes. Winner of 2020 Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress (Laura Dern).
Very emotional storytelling and great casting
Great, but not as emotional as I thought it would be
I regret wasting my time on this, don't let the positive reviews fool you; this is just two hours of two people screaming at each other with no actual story or resolution.
The kid was'nt even worth the fight.
A child victim of the parents vanity

Filme mais ou menos, o roteiro é fraco, o filme tem cenas bacanas de drama entre o casal adam driver e a scarlett johansson que salvaram o filme de ser ruim, o elenco não ajudou quase nada, e o filme quase não teve cenas relevantes para melhorar o filme.

Whilst clearly a very well made movie technically, the main focus is on Scarlett and Adam and their powerhouse performances. Both gives amazing shows whilst all the supporting actors are just as great. I felt the story was ok but seemed to lack the punch until the end. Whilst I'm sure the intention was a slow spiral at watching what divorce can do to families, something just felt, I don't know, a bit empty for the most part. I wouldn't say it's a bad movie, just probably isn't for me

A very sad movie about a harsh reality that a lot of people go through: Divorce. With Adam Driver and Scarlett Johansson as leads, it relies on a feel between theater and cinema, which is excellently done as it mirrors both characters and what they want and desire. With clever wordage, monologues, and an interesting plot without much clear action or excitement, it still delivers a heart wrecking and emotional performance of two lovers falling away from each other. The parallelism and the subtleties of every scene, and how the characters react to each other and to others made the film super lively, which meant it hurt all the more with the message they portrayed. A very good film indeed.

Marriage Story - 9/10. Marriage Story is a story that has been done before. But what makes the film different from other divorce films such as Kramer vs Kramer is the use of comedy and creating a lighter visual experience. Don't get it wrong though, Marriage Story is a sad film overall, but it's the comedy that helps alleviate the pain of seeing people fall out of love. Scarlett Johansson and Adam Driver do a splendid job of digging into their characters and providing growth throughout the duration of the film. They display a variety of emotions and play their characters in a very grounded nature. Laura Dern is a definite standout as the cocky but well intentioned divorce lawyer for Scarlett's character in the film. Azhy Robertson, who plays the son of the couple, does a great job of playing the child who just can't seem to grasp the magnitude of what his parents are going through. The real star of the movie however is definitely Noah Baumbach. He delivers a story that could have been dreadful to watch, but provides it in such a way that by the time the movie ends, it leaves in a bittersweet mood. A delightfully sad film, go watch it once it's up on Netflix! One of my favourites during the festival!

The story and acting was very realistic, and I got a little in my feels during but at the end I was so emotional and sobbed. Incredible story telling, it felt like my own story was being told.. Beautiful movie.
Excellent acting and a story that navigates the difficulties of family and divorce.

Though it can just feel like 2 hours of a couple arguing, Marriage Story is another good work from Baumbach. It slumps a lot in the middle but it has an amazing opening, and picks up a lot in the last act, enough to save the film. I didn't really like Scarlet Johansson's character but the Adam Driver's character and performance are great.

74%. 8/28/24. Dives into the selfishness of people in relationships. Driver's character is all about his own work as a director but gets jealous of Scarlett's acting career. You really see the end of their marriage through the ugly divorce process and custody battle. The legal scenes are pretty intense. Pretty depressing film. 1/5 rewatch.
Can't say it wasn't good acting but something just felt off. The pieces ( actors, scenery, story) are good on their own but when all mashed together? Not so great.

Should have been called Divorce Story, cause that’s what it’s about. I really hate films about couple’s fighting. For a film that is basically a couple arguing for two hours, it wasn’t terrible, mainly because the performances were so good. Adam Driver was absolutely brilliant. How he didn’t take home the Oscar is beyond me.

Excellently written and directed by Noah Baumbach with equally marvellous performances by Adam Driver and Scarlett Johansson, Marriage Story is a powerful, emotionally rich and totally genuine exploration of love and marriage that is greatly structured while balancing aspects of turbulence and heart without ever becoming overdramatic. It isn't the easiest watch, but the level of care and humanity makes this is a special film in my opinion that i'm glad i watched.
Might be the sign of a new era and style of filmmaking, it's as good as all those video essays say.

Scarlett Johansson and Adam Driver star as a married couple riddled with flaws ultimately resulting in a messy divorce told from both perspectives. As we can emphasize with both characters it makes the outcome all the more emotional. The lead performances as well as Dern's and Liotta's are nothing short of amazing and netted Dern an Oscar for Supporting Actress.
i cried 4 times very good movie
Masterclass on writing, performing, and characters

Love does not entail happiness, especially in marraige! As two parents are going through with their divorce, the hardship of raising their son in two different worlds wears them down as the complications lead to even more conflict between them. Scarlet Johansen and Adam Driver manage to bring up the raw anger boiling beneath the surface of a couple that has lost the love for each other!
The best performance of adam driver and scarlett johansson i've ever seen! Also it has amazing cinematography and it's very relatable for families going through divorce.
Raw, captivating, and dramatically tense as well as greatly written and directed. [Marriage Story] displays powerful performances from Johansson and Driver. 🍅

It is hard not to marvel at Driver and Johansson who both give truly great performances here and Baumbach who is finally able to approach this subject with nuance. The argument scene (you'll know it when you see it) is a triumph of naturally building tension.
it's hard but it's worth all the purifying catharsis
good acting subject matter is depressing boring and interesting film at same time cool seeing ray liotta in this

Probably director and writer Noah Baumbach's best offering to date, Marriage Story is an impactful look at a seemingly idyllic marriage gone bad. Theater director Charlie (Adam Driver) and his actress wife Nicole (Scarlett Johansson) live in New York with their young son. When their relationship begins to fracture, they agree to an amicable separation, which goes well until lawyers are introduced. The writing is sharp and the performances from the entire cast are impressive, but what makes the otherwise talky Marriage Story impressive are the quiet moments and poignant images – a tear welling up in an eye, a manilla envelope from a lawyer on a kitchen counter, the distance between the two main characters on a subway train. What makes the movie so difficult to watch is the fact that the two main characters are essentially good people, drawn into a horrible situation by external forces they seem unable to control.

I normally don't "go" for this kind of movie. But this movie kept me invested, mainly due to the phenomenal acting of Driver and Johansson who give a fantastic performance. Alan Alda kills it too as the lawyer who has seen it all and if far past his prime, and Dern as the b---- lawyer.

I am having a difficult time finding something positive to say about the movie. It is not because it is a movie about divorce. There have been good movies dealing with divorce that I have enjoyed such as "The Story of Us". The problem is that you never feel any chemistry between the characters and so the movie has to tell you why each character is so great. All of the characters, including the main characters, are so self absorbed that you never end up rooting for any particular character. Half of the dialogue is cringe. The scene where Charlie and Nicole argue it out in Charlie's apartment is probably the only good scene as Adam and Scarlett act it out very well. Sadly, most of the movie is boring and definitely not worth rewatching.
A little over-acted, some great moments

Totally waste of time. And talent. Trailer tells a story about deep love or something at that area, and you expect something deep with this cast but what you get is a not interesting divorce story, with really no point or anything new. Such a waste of my time. A real shame
Linda historia de amor.

What makes this movie truly exceptional are its captivating storyline and engaging dialogues. It's a classic gem that demands your full attention; every moment is worth savoring. If you immerse yourself in the intricacies of the plot, you're bound to fall in love with it.

While I thought that the film was very well acted and very well directed, the film itself was far too overlong. Also, The three lawyers, Alda, Liotta and Dern all played their usual character selves - I found nothing outside of their general characters that they always play. Johanssen & Driver did very well as did the boy that played Henry, but again, the film was simply overlong.
Really love this film. Adam Driver and Scarlett Johansson gave brilliant performances. Noah's script was also incredible.
I feel like the husband got the short end of the deal, not to mention it almost seemed like the child didn't care for his own father.

Depressing and long. I tried to finish it twice and couldn't. Obviously very talented acting, but its a boring, common story. Literally everyone now has people close to them that go through this so no need to relive it on the screen.
Most boring dribble ever ! we both walked out ..
Well written and convincingly acted. I'd rate it higher if it was more enjoyable to watch but it's a bit of a dry and wrought topic.
